claimText: Neutrinos are subatomic particles that possess zero electrical charge, a tiny mass, and interact with matter exclusively through the weak nuclear force and gravity.
evidenceExcerpt: Neutrinos have zero electrical charge, tiny mass... interact with matter only via weak nuclear force and gravity, and are undisturbed by magnetic fields.
examUse: Statement-based question on the fundamental properties of neutrinos.
distractorHooks: Neutrinos are deflected by magnetic fields

claimText: Neutrinos are the second-most abundant particle in the universe, surpassed only by photons.
evidenceExcerpt: They are the second-most abundant particle in the universe after photons.
examUse: Fact-based statement in a multiple-choice question regarding particle abundance.
distractorHooks: Neutrinos are the most abundant particle in the universe
Neutrinos are less abundant than electrons

claimText: Neutrino observatories are constructed deep underground to utilize the Earth's crust as a shield against interfering particles such as muons.
evidenceExcerpt: Neutrino observatories are built underground because the Earth's crust helps block interfering particles like muons.
examUse: Assertion-Reason question explaining the location of neutrino observatories.
distractorHooks: Observatories are built at high altitudes to capture cosmic rays
Underground construction is to avoid atmospheric pressure

claimText: Major global neutrino observatories include JUNO in China, INO in India, IceCube at the South Pole, and KATRIN in Germany.
evidenceExcerpt: Key observatories include JUNO (China), INO (Tamil Nadu, India), IceCube (South Pole), and KATRIN (Germany).
examUse: Correct-pair matching question associating observatories with their respective locations.
distractorHooks: IceCube is located at the North Pole

claimText: There are three types of neutrinos: Electron, Muon, and Tau, and the JUNO observatory aims to determine their mass hierarchy.
evidenceExcerpt: China's JUNO observatory aims to determine the mass hierarchy of the three neutrino types (Electron, Muon, and Tau).
examUse: Statement-based question on neutrino flavors and specific observatory objectives.
distractorHooks: There are only two types of neutrinos
JUNO aims to study gravitational waves
INO aims to determine the mass hierarchy
